top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Pedal IMUA's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before December.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
5,800ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
94ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Pedal IMUA's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Pedal IMUA. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,800ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
At Pedal IMUA, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Base fitness for Pedal IMUA. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At Pedal IMUA, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Pedal IMUA’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and VO2max to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Maui, Hawaii, USA.
